corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: we introduce noisynet a deep reinforcement learning agent with parametric noise added to its weights and show that the induced stochasticity of the agent s policy can be used to aid efficient exploration the parameters of the noise are learned with gradient descent along with the remaining network weights noisynet is straightforward to implement and adds little computational overhead we find that replacing the conventional exploration heuristics for a3c dqn and dueling agents entropy reward and -greedy respectively with noisynet yields substantially higher scores for a wide range of atari games in some cases advancing the agent from sub to super-human performance body

top_candidates.sentence: A similar randomisation technique can also be applied to LSTM units and is easily extended to reinforcement learning, we leave this as future work.

We introduce NoisyNet, a deep reinforcement learning agent with parametric noise added to its weights, and show that the induced stochasticity of the agent's policy can be used to aid efficient exploration.
We propose a simple alternative approach, called NoisyNet, where learned perturbations of the network weights are used to drive exploration.
At a high level our algorithm is a randomised value function, where the functional form is a neural network.
NoisyNet can also be adapted to any deep RL algorithm and we demonstrate this versatility by providing NoisyNet versions of DQN , Dueling and A3C algorithms.
A3C is an asynchronous and online algorithm that uses roll-outs of size k + 1 of the current policy to perform a policy improvement step.
For simplicity, here we present the A3C version with only one thread.

As mentioned previously, in the original A3C algorithm, it is recommended to add an entropy term β k i=0 ∇ θπ H(π(•|x t+i ; θ π )) to the policy update, where H(π(•|x t+i ; θ π )) = -β a∈A π(a|x t+i ; θ π ) log(π(a|x t+i ; θ π )).
As A3C is an on-policy algorithm, this involves fixing the noise of the network for the whole roll-out so that the policy produced by the network is also fixed.
Another advantage of NoisyNet is that the amount of noise injected in the network is tuned automatically by the RL algorithm.
